lesson: When product is soupy but the refrigeration is running non-stop, check the actual temp against the machine's sensor. If they don't match, the thermistor has drifted and is lying to the controller.
symptoms: Froyo is coming out like soup, but only on the middle tap.
No error codes on the display.

tests.temp-probe: Independent probe reads the middle barrel in the mid-40s °F — while the panel claims it's holding 16 °F.
tests.inspect-beater: One barrel's product sits warm and liquid while its neighbours hold texture. Nothing binding in any of the beaters.
tests.continuity-test: Middle barrel thermistor reads 5.2 kΩ at an actual 42 °F — the curve says it should be near 15 kΩ.

flavour: Thermistor was lying to the CPU. The new one tells the icy truth.
authenticityNote: Thermistors can drift due to moisture ingress or thermal cycling. If they drift to read 'cold' when the barrel is warm, the controller won't engage the cooling valve, leaving the froyo melted.
